results.possibleIdentity: Ian A. Fraser was a Canadian engineer active during the late 1960s and 1970s, based in the Toronto area. He graduated from the University of Toronto in 1963 with a focus on electrical engineering. According to his university record, he was involved with the University of Toronto Schools, the Judo Club, and the water polo team, and he expressed a intent to pursue post-graduate work specializing in Control Systems. He subsequently worked for Sinclair Radio Labs Limited, an engineering firm founded by University of Toronto professor Dr. George Sinclair, which operated a facility in Maple, Ontario. Fraser was associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization that facilitated collaborations between the scientific/engineering community and artists. His technical profile for E.A.T. listed expertise in diverse fields including control systems, bio-engineering, medicine, lasers, holography, instrumentation, 3D optics, heat-sensitive films, and visual display devices.
